framework: SOC 2 Type II — Trust Services Criteria (2017, updated 2022)
description: Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy used in SOC 2 Type II engagements.

control_families.name: Control Environment
control_families.description: The organization demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values, exercises oversight responsibility, establishes structure and authority, demonstrates commitment to competence, and enforces accountability.
example_controls: CC1.1 COSO Principle 1: Integrity and Ethical Values

control_families.name: Communication and Information
control_families.description: The organization internally and externally communicates information necessary to support the functioning of internal control, including objectives, responsibilities, and relevant changes.
example_controls: CC2.1 COSO Principle 13: Use of Relevant Information

control_families.name: Risk Assessment
control_families.description: The organization specifies objectives, identifies and analyzes risk, and assesses the potential for fraud or other threats.

control_families.name: Monitoring Activities
control_families.description: The organization selects, develops, and performs ongoing evaluations to ascertain whether controls are present and functioning, and communicates deficiencies in a timely manner.
example_controls: CC4.1 COSO Principle 16: Ongoing and Separate Evaluations

control_families.name: Control Activities
control_families.description: The organization selects and develops control activities that mitigate risks, including policies, procedures, IT general controls, and technology-based controls.
example_controls: CC5.1 COSO Principle 10: Select and Develop Control Activities
CC5.3 Deploy Through Policies and Procedures

control_families.name: Logical and Physical Access Controls
control_families.description: The organization implements logical and physical access controls to protect information assets, restrict access to authorized users, and manage credentials and authentication.
example_controls: CC6.6 Encryption of Data in Transit

control_families.name: System Operations
control_families.description: The organization manages system operations including detection and monitoring of security events, incident response, and recovery procedures.
example_controls: CC7.2 Monitor for Anomalies and Security Events

control_families.name: Change Management
control_families.description: The organization authorizes, designs, develops, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures.
example_controls: CC8.1 Authorize, Design, Develop, Test, and Implement Changes

control_families.name: Risk Mitigation
control_families.description: The organization identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities including vendor management and business continuity.
example_controls: CC9.2 Vendor and Business Partner Risk

control_families.name: Availability
control_families.description: The organization maintains, monitors, and evaluates processing capacity, environmental protections, backup, and recovery to meet availability commitments and requirements.

control_families.name: Processing Integrity
control_families.description: The organization uses processing integrity controls to ensure system processing is complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized.
example_controls: PI1.5 Processing Integrity Inputs and Outputs

control_families.name: Confidentiality
control_families.description: The organization identifies, classifies, and protects confidential information through its lifecycle, including retention and secure disposal.
example_controls: C1.1 Identify and Classify Confidential Information

control_families.name: Privacy
control_families.description: The organization provides notice, obtains consent, collects only necessary personal information, and manages retention, disposal, disclosure, and access to personal information.
